steps.title: Identify the contrast
steps.content: The passage moves from describing what people initially assume—a real hole with sculptures—to what's actually true: an illusion of depth. The transition must signal this shift from false impression to reality.

steps.title: Select the transition word that marks opposition
steps.content: ”On the contrary” directly announces a shift from one perspective to its opposite. The other three suggest addition, sequence, or causation rather than contrast. This matches choice D — D is correct.
